page: Microsoft Azure F8as_v6 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the Fasv6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz, configured with 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core metrics placing in the top 10% and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Multi-core performance across stress-ng and Geekbench remains in the average tier. A notable hardware tradeoff is present in memory bandwidth: while cached small-block read operations perform at average speeds, uncached large-block read memory bandwidth is weak, falling into the bottom 25%. Text processing and small-model LLM text generation demonstrate strong performance, while database operations, static web serving, and software compilation align with average tier baselines.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F8as_v6 offers a balanced resource profile with dedicated CPU allocation, avoiding the performance variability of shared-core alternatives. This configuration is suitable for workloads requiring high single-thread performance alongside moderate memory capacity, such as web serving, text processing, software compilation, and lightweight language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F8as_v6 is an x86_64 virtual machine in the Fasv6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores, 8 vCPUs, and 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. This server does not include local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks highlight top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core tests, alongside strong text processing and small-model LLM text generation. However, a key tradeoff is its uncached large-block read memory bandwidth, which falls into the bottom 25%. Multi-core compute, database operations, and static web serving perform within average ranges. This instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, web serving, software compilation, and applications requiring high single-threaded performance without massive memory or local storage requirements.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8as_v6 is an 8-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor, 32.0 GB of RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, suited for general-purpose and web serving workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8as_v6 features 8 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 32.0 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eight-core AMD EPYC virtual machine delivering top-tier single-threaded performance and balanced memory for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Eight dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU cores running at 3.7 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 10%
Supports Premium Storage with no local storage or integrated GPUs
Suited for web serving, text processing, and software compilation
